title: Fermented Indian Lamb Curry
description: Snappy, flavorful, and nutritious meal-prep dish featuring lamb, dried apricots, and fermented spices. This Ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner or special occasion.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the lamb, 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, onion, ginger, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, garam masala, and salt. Mix well with your hands until the lamb is evenly coated with the spice blend.
Refrigerate the lamb mixture for at least 2 hours or overnight to allow the flavors to meld.
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the lamb mixture and cook until the lamb is browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the lamb mixture to a slow cooker and add the rehydrated apricots and their liquid. Cook on low for 6-8 hours or high for 3-4 hours.
After cooking, use two forks to shred the lamb into bite-sized pieces. Season with salt to taste and serve hot over basmati rice or with naan bread.
Ferment the curry in an airtight container at room temperature for 24-48 hours to develop a tangy, snappy flavor.
Before serving, store the curry in an airtight container in the refrigerator and allow it to chill for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
